response.reasoning: The role is a Director-level IC Principal Engineer focused on AI strategy, requiring 15 years of experience, recognized AI expertise, cloud fluency, and the ability to advise strategic customers and inspire at scale. The candidate has roughly 14-16 years of professional software development experience (since ~2009-2012 depending on how freelance counts), primarily as a hands-on senior/staff full-stack developer across web, mobile, embedded, and GIS domains. Strengths include broad polyglot engineering, TDD/craftsmanship discipline, founding an open-source GIS platform, public speaking/workshops, and recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure (MCP code generators) at re:cinq. However, the AI/ML experience is very recent and shallow relative to a Principal-level AI strategy role — no published AI products, no ML research background, no PhD/Master's in AI. Cloud experience exists (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) but is not deep platform-level. There is no evidence of director-level influence, advising strategic enterprise customers, or shaping multi-team technical strategy. Title trajectory is Senior/Staff, not Principal. Overall a strong senior craftsman but a stretch for a Principal AI Strategy role.
strengths: Broad 14+ year polyglot engineering background across web, mobile, embedded, and infra
Founder of open-source GIS platform with real users, plus active community speaking/teaching
Recent hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq
concerns: AI/ML depth is thin and very recent — no demonstrated AI products or platforms at scale
No prior Principal/Director-level scope or experience advising strategic enterprise customers
Cloud experience is applied (AWS CDK, K8s) rather than platform-strategy level

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise and shipped AI products
Seniority gap vs. Principal/Director IC scope

response.justification: The candidate is a capable senior/staff engineer with strong craftsmanship, community presence, and emerging exposure to AI-agent tooling, but the role demands recognized AI expertise, prior principal-level influence, and the ability to advise strategic customers on cloud/AI futures. The resume does not demonstrate that scope or that AI depth, so they are unlikely to clear the bar for a Principal AI Strategy IC, though a screening conversation could clarify the depth of recent AI-agent work.
